title: oVirt / RHV VDSM Host Reconnect Storm and Storage Lease Errors
dataSources: `index=virtualization` with primary `sourcetype="ovirt:vdsm"` from `/var/log/vdsm/vdsm.log` (Python/threads: **vds:Client**, Reactor, storage helpers, `libvirt` errors, xmlrpc/jsonrpc, sanlock callouts). Optional: `sourcetype="ovirt:sanlock"` for `/var/log/sanlock.log`, `sourcetype="ovirt:engine"` or `ovirt:audit` for “Failed to communicate with VDSM” style correlation, `sourcetype="linux:journald"` for `vdsmd` / `vdsm` when file paths are off-limits. Field names vary by oVirt 4.4+ vs 4.5+ and by forwarder: sample five events and align `EXTRACT-` and `coalesce(log_level, level, severity, …)` with what you see.
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder (or Heavy Forwarder) on every oVirt / RHV hypervisor with `monitor://` to `/var/log/vdsm/vdsm.log` into `index=virtualization`, `sourcetype=ovirt:vdsm`, plus optional monitors for `sanlock.log` and engine-side logs if you want cross-source rows. There is no requirement for a Splunkbase oVirt/RHV add-on: ship a small custom app (for example `TA-ovirt-vdsm-local`) with the `inputs.conf` and `props.conf` from this use case. Red Hat Insights for RHEL (Splunkbase) is adjacent telemetry for the OS, not a substitute for VDSM file paths.
spl:
index=virtualization (sourcetype="ovirt:vdsm" OR sourcetype="ovirt:sanlock" OR sourcetype="ovirt:engine" OR sourcetype="ovirt:audit" OR sourcetype="linux:journald") earliest=-1h
| eval src_kind=case(sourcetype="ovirt:vdsm","vdsm", sourcetype="ovirt:sanlock","sanlock", sourcetype="ovirt:engine","engine", sourcetype="ovirt:audit","audit", sourcetype="linux:journald","journald", 1=1,"other")
| eval log_lvl=upper(trim(tostring(coalesce(log_level, level, severity, loglevel, logLevel, ""))))
| rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)host(?:name)?\\s*[:=]\\s*(?<h_msg>[A-Za-z0-9._-]+)"
| eval hostkey=trim(tostring(coalesce(nullif(host,""), nullif(Computer,""), nullif(nodename,""), h_msg)))
| eval hostkey=if(isnull(hostkey) OR hostkey="" OR match(hostkey,"^(?i)null$"), if(src_kind IN ("engine","audit"),"engine-aggregate","unassigned"), hostkey)
| eval is_reconnect=if(match(_raw, "(?i)(vds:Client|vdsm\\.client)\\b.{0,360}(?i)(disconnected|disconnect|re-?connect|reconnect|connection\\s*(?:reset|lost|refused|closed|aborted))"),1,0)
| eval is_reconnect=if(is_reconnect=0 AND match(_raw, "(?i)(connect|reconnect|session).{0,120}(?i)(vds:Client|VDSM\\s+client)"),1,is_reconnect)
| eval is_lease_err=if(match(_raw, "(?i)(attachStorageDomain|getVolumeSize|detachStorageDomain|setStorageDomain|VM\\s+lease|image\\s+lease|storage\\s*domain).{0,220}(?i)(fail|error|exception|IOError|cannot|unable|errno|timeout|not\\s+found)"),1,0)
| eval is_lease_err=if(is_lease_err=0 AND match(_raw, "(?i)connectStoragePool.{0,200}(?i)(fail|error|cannot|unable|timeout|exception)"),1,is_lease_err)
| eval is_spmish=if(match(_raw, "(?i)(getSpmStatus|IrsBroker|lockSpace|SPM_ACQUIRE|Failed to acquire\\s+SPM|Spm Status)"),1,0)
| eval is_sanlock_err=if(match(_raw, "(?i)sanlock.{0,200}(?i)(add_lockspace|acquire|host_id|host id|delta lease)"),1,0)
| eval is_libvirt_err=if(match(_raw, "(?i)(libvirtError|libvirt\\.libvirtError|Cannot find storage pool|Failed to acquire lock|virConnect)"),1,0)
| eval is_timeout=if(match(_raw, "(?i)(xmlrpc|jsonrpc|JsonRpcServer|jsonrpc|task).{0,180}(?i)(timeout|timed out|deadline)"),1,0)
| eval is_neterr=if(match(_raw, "(?i)(vds:NetworkError|vds:NetworkException|ConnectionError|NetworkError).{0,200}(?i)(storage|iscsi|nfs|mount|path)"),1,0)
| eval is_eng_vdsm=if(src_kind IN ("engine","audit") AND match(_raw, "(?i)(Failed to communicate with VDSM|VDSM.*(unreachable|not\\s+responding|connection\\s*refused))"),1,0)
| bin _time span=5m aligntime=earliest
| stats sum(is_reconnect) as n_reconnect, sum(is_lease_err) as n_lease_err, sum(is_sanlock_err) as n_sanlock_err, sum(is_libvirt_err) as n_libvirt_err, sum(is_timeout) as n_timeout, sum(is_neterr) as n_neterr, sum(is_eng_vdsm) as n_eng_vdsm, sum(is_spmish) as n_spmish_hits, dc(src_kind) as src_dc, max(len(_raw)) as maxraw by _time, hostkey
| rename hostkey as host
| eval severity_bucket=case(n_reconnect>=3 AND n_lease_err>=1, "critical", n_reconnect>=3 OR n_lease_err>=1, "warning", n_reconnect>0 OR n_lease_err>0 OR n_sanlock_err>0 OR n_libvirt_err>0 OR n_timeout>0 OR n_neterr>0 OR n_eng_vdsm>0, "watch", 1=1, "ok")
| eval cross_hint=if(n_eng_vdsm>0 AND n_reconnect>0, 1, 0)
| eval spm_routing=if(n_spmish_hits>=2 AND n_reconnect<2 AND n_lease_err<1, "check_UC-19.1.42_SPM_context", null())
| sort 0 - _time, severity_bucket, n_reconnect
| table _time, host, n_reconnect, n_lease_err, n_sanlock_err, n_libvirt_err, n_timeout, n_neterr, n_eng_vdsm, n_spmish_hits, cross_hint, spm_routing, severity_bucket, src_dc, maxraw
description: This detector targets oVirt 4.4+ / Red Hat Virtualization 4.4+ hypervisor agent behaviour in `vdsm.log` (and optional `sanlock.log`, engine, audit, journal) using a `src_kind` field per canonical sourcetype, defensive `coalesce` on `log_level` / `level` / `severity`, a regex bank for management-channel churn (`vds:Client` connect and disconnect on the Reactor path), non-coordinator storage-domain failures (for example `attachStorageDomain`, `getVolumeSize`, `connectStoragePool` with failure text, generic `IOError` and lease-like phrases on data domains without requiring Storage Pool Manager election strings), `sanlock` `add_lockspace` / `host_id` style failures, `libvirt` pool and lock errors, xmlrpc/jsonrpc and task timeouts, and `vds:NetworkError` when proximate to storage vocabulary. It bins `_time` to five minutes and rolls up per extracted `host` (or `engine-aggregate` for engine lines without a resolvable host token) to emit `n_reconnect`, `n_lease_err`, and companion counters, then assigns `severity_bucket` in four bands: `critical` when the same host and window shows at least three reconnect-style hits and at least one lease-error hit, `warning` when either threshold alone fires, `watch` for any other positive indicator count, and `ok` when nothing matched. A parallel `n_spmish_hits` counter tags lines that look like the SPM story so operators can hand off to UC-19.1.42 when the narrative is pure coordinator/IRS language without a reconnect storm. This is intentionally not a generic `engine.log` Java ERROR mass detector (UC-19.1.41) and not a cluster-level SPM `has_cross` rollup (UC-19.1.42).
value: When a hypervisor’s VDSM session to the oVirt or RHV Engine flaps, or when storage paths and non-SPM leases misbehave, the failure often shows in host-side logs first while the admin UI is still green. Catching a reconnect storm with lease or sanlock context shortens the path from “mysterious storage pause” to network, multipath, array, or host remediation before VMs stall en masse or the cluster starts fencing. The use case is tuned for the third axis in the oVirt set: you route pure engine Java burst noise to UC-19.1.41, SPM election and `has_cross` storage-coordinator risk to UC-19.1.42, and keep this runbook for per-host VDSM transport plus non-coordinator storage failures so incident bridges split along the right signal. The saved search is meant for virtualization platform on-call first, with storage and network on secondary bridge when `n_lease_err` and `n_neterr` stay hot across bins.
implementation: Put a Universal Forwarder on each KVM hypervisor, monitor `/var/log/vdsm/vdsm.log` to `sourcetype=ovirt:vdsm` with one-line or thread-safe `LINE_BREAKER` rules, add optional `sanlock.log` and `engine` monitors if you need correlation, then schedule the `spl` every five minutes with lookback 65 minutes, throttle on `host` and `severity_bucket`, and open UC-19.1.42 when `spm_routing` is populated. Validate five raw lines per field family before you tune regex thresholds in production.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• oVirt Engine 4.4+ or RHV 4.4+ and KVM hypervisor hosts (RHEL, Rocky, Alma 8/9) running **vdsmd** with `/var/log/vdsm/vdsm.log` readable to the forwarder service account. Group membership: the forwarder user often needs the `vdsm` file group (or `root`-readable deploy pattern) to read the log; document your choice in the runbook. Chrony or NTP on every node: a reconnect storm in Splunk with wall-clock gaps usually means skew, not physics.
• Index `virtualization` (or your standard) and a search role for operators. Retention: long enough to compare a flapping week against a clean baseline—often 14–30 days for ops review.
• Baseline: five known-good VDSM lines per thread style (MainThread, Reactor, task worker) and five errors from a lab, so you can re-home `EXTRACT-` and `LINE_BREAKER` if an upgrade reorders tokens.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**

Install Universal Forwarder on every hypervisor. Create an app, for example `TA-ovirt-vdsm-local`, with `local/inputs.conf` as below. The path is the default; if you relocate `var`, adjust once and mirror in documentation.

```ini
[monitor:///var/log/vdsm/vdsm.log]
disabled = 0
host = $decideOnStartup
crcSalt = <SOURCE>
index = virtualization
sourcetype = ovirt:vdsm

# Optional: same host, sanlock daemon text
[monitor:///var/log/sanlock.log]
disabled = 0
host = $decideOnStartup
crcSalt = <SOURCE>
index = virtualization
sourcetype = ovirt:sanlock
```

`props.conf` for VDSM should treat each logical log record as a single event. VDSM often prefixes lines with a thread or logger token; the goal is a breaker that starts a new event where the timestamp for that thread begins, not in the middle of a Python stack. Start from this baseline and then paste two real `vdsm.log` headers from your build to confirm.

```ini
[ovirt:vdsm]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
# Break on newline before a new timestamp; adjust if your file begins with a short hostname before the date.
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)(?:[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+\s+)?\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\s+\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}
TRUNCATE = 1000000
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
TIME_PREFIX = ^
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S,%3N
# If your line prints a timezone offset after the comma, add %%z to TIME_FORMAT or set TZ on the UF to match the writer.

# Optional: when the TA or props layer names fields, keep names stable across upgrades.
# EXTRACT-vdsm_line = <your build-specific regex; sample 5 events first — oVirt 4.5+ may reorder tokens>
```

`ovirt:sanlock` (if enabled):

```ini
[ovirt:sanlock]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TRUNCATE = 200000
TIME_PREFIX = ^
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S
# Some builds include milliseconds: switch to %Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S,%3N if your sample lines show a comma before ms.
```

Permissions: if `vdsm.log` is `root:vdsm` mode 0640, add the `splunk` user to the `vdsm` group on RHEL-derivatives (`usermod -aG vdsm splunk` then service restart) or use a controlled ACL; avoid world-readable permission changes on production without change control. For Engine-side “Failed to communicate with VDSM” correlation, re-use the engine `monitor` stanzas from UC-19.1.41 / UC-19.1.42 so sourcetype names stay `ovirt:engine` and `ovirt:audit`—do not introduce ad hoc `ovirt:vdsmd` style typos. There is no mandatory Splunkbase oVirt add-on: community or internal TAs must still apply these props on the first parsing tier. If you already ship host logs through **Settings → Data inputs** on a Heavy Forwarder, keep sourcetype names identical so this SPL does not fork.

**Step 2 — Build the search**

Run the following SPL as a saved report or the basis of a scheduled search. The `spl` field in this JSON matches; edit one and mirror the other. The search keeps `_raw` regexes so a half-built `EXTRACT-` on upgrade day does not zero out the runbook, but you should still add extractions for dashboards. The `coalesce` on severity-like fields is there for TAs and HF transforms that map `level` to `log_level` inconsistently. The `h_msg` `rex` backfills a host token from a fraction of engine lines; when it cannot, engine-side rows land on `host=engine-aggregate` so you still see the cluster narrative without faking a hypervisor FQDN. After the five-minute `stats`, `severity_bucket` encodes: `critical` (≥3 reconnects and ≥1 lease-class hit in the same bin and host), `warning` (≥3 reconnects or ≥1 lease-class hit), `watch` (any other positive component counter), and `ok` (no matches). The `n_spmish_hits` flag helps you decide whether UC-19.1.42 is the better primary story. Remove `linux:journald` from the first line if you do not have mixed ingestion.

```spl
index=virtualization (sourcetype="ovirt:vdsm" OR sourcetype="ovirt:sanlock" OR sourcetype="ovirt:engine" OR sourcetype="ovirt:audit" OR sourcetype="linux:journald") earliest=-1h
| eval src_kind=case(sourcetype="ovirt:vdsm","vdsm", sourcetype="ovirt:sanlock","sanlock", sourcetype="ovirt:engine","engine", sourcetype="ovirt:audit","audit", sourcetype="linux:journald","journald", 1=1,"other")
| eval log_lvl=upper(trim(tostring(coalesce(log_level, level, severity, loglevel, logLevel, ""))))
| rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)host(?:name)?\\s*[:=]\\s*(?<h_msg>[A-Za-z0-9._-]+)"
| eval hostkey=trim(tostring(coalesce(nullif(host,""), nullif(Computer,""), nullif(nodename,""), h_msg)))
| eval hostkey=if(isnull(hostkey) OR hostkey="" OR match(hostkey,"^(?i)null$"), if(src_kind IN ("engine","audit"),"engine-aggregate","unassigned"), hostkey)
| eval is_reconnect=if(match(_raw, "(?i)(vds:Client|vdsm\\.client)\\b.{0,360}(?i)(disconnected|disconnect|re-?connect|reconnect|connection\\s*(?:reset|lost|refused|closed|aborted))"),1,0)
| eval is_reconnect=if(is_reconnect=0 AND match(_raw, "(?i)(connect|reconnect|session).{0,120}(?i)(vds:Client|VDSM\\s+client)"),1,is_reconnect)
| eval is_lease_err=if(match(_raw, "(?i)(attachStorageDomain|getVolumeSize|detachStorageDomain|setStorageDomain|VM\\s+lease|image\\s+lease|storage\\s*domain).{0,220}(?i)(fail|error|exception|IOError|cannot|unable|errno|timeout|not\\s+found)"),1,0)
| eval is_lease_err=if(is_lease_err=0 AND match(_raw, "(?i)connectStoragePool.{0,200}(?i)(fail|error|cannot|unable|timeout|exception)"),1,is_lease_err)
| eval is_spmish=if(match(_raw, "(?i)(getSpmStatus|IrsBroker|lockSpace|SPM_ACQUIRE|Failed to acquire\\s+SPM|Spm Status)"),1,0)
| eval is_sanlock_err=if(match(_raw, "(?i)sanlock.{0,200}(?i)(add_lockspace|acquire|host_id|host id|delta lease)"),1,0)
| eval is_libvirt_err=if(match(_raw, "(?i)(libvirtError|libvirt\\.libvirtError|Cannot find storage pool|Failed to acquire lock|virConnect)"),1,0)
| eval is_timeout=if(match(_raw, "(?i)(xmlrpc|jsonrpc|JsonRpcServer|jsonrpc|task).{0,180}(?i)(timeout|timed out|deadline)"),1,0)
| eval is_neterr=if(match(_raw, "(?i)(vds:NetworkError|vds:NetworkException|ConnectionError|NetworkError).{0,200}(?i)(storage|iscsi|nfs|mount|path)"),1,0)
| eval is_eng_vdsm=if(src_kind IN ("engine","audit") AND match(_raw, "(?i)(Failed to communicate with VDSM|VDSM.*(unreachable|not\\s+responding|connection\\s*refused))"),1,0)
| bin _time span=5m aligntime=earliest
| stats sum(is_reconnect) as n_reconnect, sum(is_lease_err) as n_lease_err, sum(is_sanlock_err) as n_sanlock_err, sum(is_libvirt_err) as n_libvirt_err, sum(is_timeout) as n_timeout, sum(is_neterr) as n_neterr, sum(is_eng_vdsm) as n_eng_vdsm, sum(is_spmish) as n_spmish_hits, dc(src_kind) as src_dc, max(len(_raw)) as maxraw by _time, hostkey
| rename hostkey as host
| eval severity_bucket=case(n_reconnect>=3 AND n_lease_err>=1, "critical", n_reconnect>=3 OR n_lease_err>=1, "warning", n_reconnect>0 OR n_lease_err>0 OR n_sanlock_err>0 OR n_libvirt_err>0 OR n_timeout>0 OR n_neterr>0 OR n_eng_vdsm>0, "watch", 1=1, "ok")
| eval cross_hint=if(n_eng_vdsm>0 AND n_reconnect>0, 1, 0)
| eval spm_routing=if(n_spmish_hits>=2 AND n_reconnect<2 AND n_lease_err<1, "check_UC-19.1.42_SPM_context", null())
| sort 0 - _time, severity_bucket, n_reconnect
| table _time, host, n_reconnect, n_lease_err, n_sanlock_err, n_libvirt_err, n_timeout, n_neterr, n_eng_vdsm, n_spmish_hits, cross_hint, spm_routing, severity_bucket, src_dc, maxraw
```

**Step 3 — Validate the results**

(a) On a hypervisor, run `sudo tail -F /var/log/vdsm/vdsm.log` and watch for `vds:Client` and storage-domain error tokens during a harmless UI refresh; the Splunk `host` for that forwarder should match the FQDN you expect, or the inventory name you will join later.

(b) `sudo vdsm-client Host getStats` (older builds may use `vdsClient` with similar verbs) to confirm the host is healthy while you compare a five-minute bin that shows `n_reconnect>0`—a pure Splunk path bug often shows as zero in VDSM while the terminal still logs reconnects. If the CLI is clean but Splunk is hot, re-check `LINE_BREAKER` splitting lines so reconnect phrases stay inside one event.

(c) `sudo journalctl -u vdsmd -S -1h --no-pager` on the same host. Count obvious errors by eye, then in Splunk run a narrow time window and compare order of magnitude: you want story-level agreement, not byte-identical lines.

(d) Sibling: UC-19.1.41 (engine ERROR/SEVERE mass) can page while this search’s `n_reconnect` is zero; open engine logs, not this host runbook, when the Java `logger` classes dominate. UC-19.1.42 (SPM) often shows `n_spmish_hits` and engine-side IRS language with cluster-wide `has_cross` logic—use that UC when the Events tab narrates Storage Pool Manager election, not a per-host `vds:Client` flap alone.

(e) In Administration Portal → Hosts pick the suspect host, compare the UI non-operational transition time to the first `warning` or `critical` bin here; minor skew is common when the API lags the log by tens of seconds.

**Step 4 — Operationalize as alert + dashboard**

Saved search: **Settings → Searches, reports, and alerts** → create a scheduled search with cron `*/5 * * * *`, `earliest = -65m@m`, `latest = now`, and trigger on `severity_bucket` in ("warning","critical") for paging. Add a secondary alert for `severity_bucket=watch` to a low-noise channel during the first month of rollout. Throttle: thirty minutes on `host` and `severity_bucket` so the same flapping link does not page every five minutes. For webhooks, include `_time`, `host`, all `n_*` columns, `cross_hint` (engine-to-host story), and `spm_routing` (pointer to the SPM use case) so the bridge can split ownership.

Dashboard layout: row one, single value for worst `severity_bucket` in the last hour and a `timechart` of `n_reconnect` stacked by `host` (top ten series). Row two, overlay `n_lease_err` and `n_neterr` for the noisiest host from a drilldown token. Row three, table of the last twelve five-minute bins with the full field set, colour by `severity_bucket`, drilldown to raw events: `index=virtualization (sourcetype=ovirt:vdsm OR sourcetype=ovirt:sanlock) host=$click.host$` with time bounds bound to the row’s `_time` window. Row four optional: show `ovirt:audit` `VDSM_HOST_VERSION` or `vds_state_changed` if you index audit, using a separate panel so you do not conflate with this search’s `stats` output.

**Step 5 — Troubleshoot common issues**

(1) Sourcetype typo `ovirt:vdsmd` vs the canonical `ovirt:vdsm`—zero hits in the first clause. Fix the forwarder and restart, then confirm metadata.

(2) `LINE_BREAKER` still splits Reactor or MainThread continuations: `vds:Client` and its tail land in two events and regex counts undercount. Re-sample headers; anchor the breaker to the timestamp your build prints for each thread, not to a naïve “starts with digit” only.

(3) `level` extracted from the wrong column on jsonrpc or Reactor lines while MainThread used a different layout. Use five raw `head` samples and a temporary `| rex` in search to name fields before you hard-code extractions; keep `log_lvl` as `coalesce` in production SPL.

(4) Host clock skew: bins align to Splunk time; hypervisors in the past or future clump or spread reconnects. Fix chrony first, then re-evaluate alert noise.

(5) Logrotate: a sudden burst at rotate time is often re-ingest or duplicate if `crcSalt` is missing—mirror UC-19.1.41’s `<SOURCE>` pattern.

(6) Planned host activate or re-enroll: legitimate connect/disconnect sequences during a maintenance operation can push `n_reconnect` for a few bins—use your change ticket lookup before you call a Sev-1 for storage.

(7) `sanlock.log` not collected: `n_sanlock_err` stays zero in Splunk even when `vdsm` mentions sanlock; enable the optional monitor. Double-ingest: if you also pick up the same line via `linux:journald`, counts can double; pick one path per host.

(8) EXTRACT patterns miss oVirt 4.5+ reword: upgrade lab first, then adjust regex, not the reverse; keep `_raw` as the source of truth for the alert even when extractions are wrong.

(9) Engine events without host token always bucket to `engine-aggregate` and can look like a false “cluster” signal—tighten `rex` for your site’s `host=` phrasing in engine messages, or add a `lookup` from engine event id to hypervisor FQDN if you have one in CMDB. When `spm_routing` fires but the Events tab is clearly SPM, pivot to UC-19.1.42 after you capture a screen shot for the handoff. When only `n_neterr` rises on iSCSI VLAN maintenance, link to network change records before opening a storage case.
visualization: Row 1: single value of worst `severity_bucket` in last 60m; timechart 5m `n_reconnect` by `host` (top 10). Row 2: dual axis `n_lease_err` and `n_neterr` for selected host. Row 3: table of last N bins: `_time`, `host`, all `n_*`, `cross_hint`, `spm_routing`, `severity_bucket`. Row 4 optional: audit or Events-linked panel from `ovirt:audit` with separate SPL. Drills to raw `ovirt:vdsm` and `ovirt:sanlock` for the same window.

references.title: oVirt / VDSM (GitHub: source and developer notes)
Red Hat — log collector: engine and host context
Splunk — monitor input and file path reference (UF)

hardware: KVM hypervisors managed by oVirt 4.4+ or Red Hat Virtualization 4.4+ on 64-bit RHEL, Rocky, or AlmaLinux 8/9, running **vdsmd**, libvirtd, qemu-kvm, sanlock for lock coordination, and typical enterprise NICs and Fibre Channel or iSCSI HBA with multipath. Hosts are usually rack servers (Dell PowerEdge, HPE ProLiant, or Lenovo) with 128–1024 GiB RAM and all-flash or hybrid SAN, but the use case is software-defined: the signal is the VDSM and sanlock logs, not a particular SKU.
exclusions: This use case is the third oVirt sibling: per-hypervisor VDSM and optional sanlock/ journal/ engine lines that describe host-side agent reconnects, non-Storage-Pool-Manager storage-domain path failures, sanlock `host_id` acquisition problems, and related libvirt, timeout, and network-to-storage class errors. It explicitly does not replace UC-19.1.41, which is the oVirt or RHV Engine `engine.log` Java ERROR and SEVERE mass detector with per-`logger` ranking and a purely control-plane focus—if your blast radius is JBoss, JDBC, and REST API class names without `vds:Client` or VDSM host file paths, stay on 19.1.41. It also does not replace UC-19.1.42, which is the Storage Pool Manager election, `has_cross` engine-plus-VDSM lock narrative, and SPM-specific IRS language: when `n_spmish_hits` and engine IRS strings dominate the story and the Events view talks SPM coordinator, run 19.1.42 as primary and use this use case for supporting host flaps. Standalone `libvirtd` on lab KVM without oVirt Engine is out of scope (no oVirt VDSM contract). oVirt **hyperconverged Gluster** brick health, heal splits, and GlusterFS capacity-only stories belong to storage-specific runbooks, not to this VDSM reconnect detector unless the log lines still match the regex bank. VMware vSphere, Nutanix AHV, and Microsoft Hyper-V hosts are out of scope. Proxmox VE and its `pve-cluster` / `pvedaemon` logs are a different product family. Oracle Linux Virtualization Manager (OLVM) in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure managed form is excluded because the operations plane, paths, and support model diverge. Pure network-device telemetry (switch, router) with no VDSM file is excluded: correlate at the change ticket, not in this search alone. Application-layer database tuning inside guest VMs is excluded; we watch the host agent, not SQL wait stats. Ceph RBD-only farms without oVirt in the path are out of scope. Finally, this UC is not a replacement for file-system and hardware UCs: local SSD wear, `smartd` predictive failure, and single-disk RAID rebuild copy should remain separate signals even if a burst of I/O shows up in `n_lease_err` in passing.
knownFalsePositives: • Planned host activation or manual reconnect during a change ticket that explains a short vds:Client connect and disconnect pair. • Rolling VDSM or host OS upgrade: documented reboot order can emit reconnect strings without ongoing pathology. • Intentional systemctl restart vdsmd or minor VDSM package update during a vendor-directed patch window. • Disaster-recovery rehearsal where a host is network-isolated on purpose, producing reconnect noise until the script ends. • Fence dry-run or test harness that triggers XML-RPC timeouts once and clears, visible in the same MOP. • Sanlock messages during a single post-reboot window while the host rejoins all lockspaces: pair with a short time gate before paging. • NIC bond failover: a single transient disconnect or reconnect on the management VLAN may increment n_reconnect for one bin. • iSCSI or NFS maintenance with read-only storage paths where everyone expects impact: suppress with change lookup. • Storage array microcode with transient path freeze that lasts less than one five-minute bin and self-clears in Events. • Engine-side certificate or websocket refresh that momentarily says “unreachable” to VDSM while the UI is still usable: tune n_eng_vdsm floors if you see annual noise. • Lab clusters with aggressive power management: hosts sleep and wake; production policy should exclude that index or lower severity for non-prod. • First boot after a clean install: high chatter until the host stabilizes; hold alerts for a cluster-age flag or the first 24h after expand.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the small program on each server that keeps in touch with the control room. When it keeps losing the link or the shared-disk safety checks fail, we catch that so people can fix the network or storage before machines freeze. We keep this separate from a general main-server error storm and from the special “who runs storage” problem so the right experts are called.
